title: Falafel & Hummus Platter
description: The quintessential Middle Eastern street food. Crispy-on-the-outside, herb-green-on-the-inside chickpea fritters paired with a hummus so creamy it feels like silk. Served with warm pita and a bright, zesty tabbouleh.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Dried Chickpeas","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"soaked overnight (never canned)"},{"name":"Tahini","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"high-quality Lebanese style"},{"name":"Fresh Parsley & Cilantro","qty":"2","unit":"bunches","notes":"for the vibrance"},{"name":"Sumac & Cumin","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"for the earthy citrus pop"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Falafel**: Pulse soaked chickpeas with herbs, garlic, and onions until they resemble coarse sand. Do not puree. Refrigerate for 1 hour to let the starches bind.
2. **The Silk (Hummus)**: Boil chickpeas with a pinch of baking soda until they are mushy. Blend while hot with cold water, tahini, and lemon juice until magically smooth.
3. **The Fry**: Scoop falafel into small balls and fry in hot oil until deeply browned. Assemble the platter with hummus, a pool of olive oil, and a sprinkle of sumac.
cookwareJson: ["Food processor", "High-speed blender", "Deep fryer or heavy pot", "Slotted spoon"]

title: Shakshuka
description: A vibrant North African and Levantine breakfast staple. Perfectly poached eggs nestled in a simmering, spiced tomato and bell pepper sauce. It's a one-pan wonder that invites communal dipping with crusty bread.
stepsMd:
1. **The Base**: Sauté onions and peppers until soft. Add garlic, harissa, and tomatoes. Simmer until the sauce reduces into a thick, concentrated stew.
2. **The Poach**: Make small wells in the sauce with a spoon. Carefully crack an egg into each well. Cover the pan and let the whites set while the yolks remain jammy.
3. **Finish**: Sprinkle with crumbled feta, fresh cilantro, and a heavy pinch of za'atar. Serve straight from the pan with charred sourdough.
cookwareJson: ["Cast iron skillet or wide shallow pan", "Metal lid"]

title: Shawarma Wrap
description: Thinly sliced, spice-marinated chicken breast or thigh, seared to a crisp and wrapped in a soft pita. Exploding with the flavors of garlic sauce (toum), pickled turnips, and fresh garden vegetables.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Chicken Thighs","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"boneless"},{"name":"Shawarma Spice Mix","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Cumin, coriander, turmeric, cloves"},{"name":"Garlic (for Toum)","qty":"1","unit":"head","notes":"the secret ingredient"},{"name":"Pickled Turnips","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"bright pink and crunchy"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Marinade**: Marinate chicken in spices, yogurt, and lemon for at least 4 hours. This ensures the meat remains juicy even when seared at high heat.
2. **The Toum**: Create a garlic emulsion by slowly whisking oil into mashed garlic and lemon juice—it should look like fluffy white clouds.
3. **The Sear**: Pan-sear chicken in a smoking hot skillet until the edges are crispy and charred. Shred finely. Wrap in pita with toum, veggies, and pickles, then toast the entire wrap on the griddle for a final crunch.
cookwareJson: ["Heavy skillet", "High-speed blender or mortar/pestle for toum", "Tongs"]

title: Texas Style Beef Brisket
description: A low-and-slow barbecue titan. Beef brisket is seasoned simply with 'Dalmatian rub' (salt and pepper) and smoked until it develops a majestic black bark and a tender, melt-in-your-mouth interior.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Beef Brisket","qty":"3","unit":"kg","notes":"Prime grade, well-marbled"},{"name":"Coarse Salt & Black Pepper","qty":"1/2","unit":"cup","notes":"50/50 ratio"},{"name":"Post Oak or Hickory Wood","qty":"1","unit":"bag","notes":"for the smoke"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Trim**: Trim the fat cap to exactly 1/4 inch. This allows the smoke to penetrate while self-basting the meat. Apply the salt and pepper rub generously.
2. **The Smoke**: Maintain your smoker at a steady 110°C (225°F). Cook until the internal temperature reaches 74°C (165°F)—the 'stall'.
3. **The Wrap**: Wrap in peach butcher paper (never foil) to preserve the bark while accelerating the final cook to 95°C (203°F). Rest for at least 2 hours in a warm cooler. The meat should wobble like jelly.
cookwareJson: ["Offset smoker or pellet grill", "Meat thermometer", "Peach butcher paper", "Sharp slicing knife"]

title: New England Clam Chowder
description: The definitive American seafood stew. A thick, cream-based broth loaded with tender clams, salty bacon lardons, and buttery potatoes. Served with oyster crackers for a classic coastal vibe.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Fresh Littleneck Clams","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"scrubbed"},{"name":"Heavy Cream","qty":"200","unit":"ml","notes":"for richness"},{"name":"Salt Pork or Bacon","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"for the fat base"},{"name":"Russet Potatoes","qty":"2","unit":"large","notes":"diced into cubes"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Base**: Render bacon until crispy. Sauté onions and celery in the fat. Add a bit of flour to make a roux—this is the 'thickening' secret.
2. **The Clams**: Steam clams separately in a little white wine until they pop open. Reserve the juice—this 'clam liquor' is the liquid gold that flavors the soup.
3. **The Simmer**: Add clam liquor, stock, potatoes, and cream. Simmer until potatoes are fork-tender. Add the clam meat at the very last second to ensure it stays tender, not rubbery.
cookwareJson: ["Heavy stock pot", "Small pot for steaming", "Fine-mesh sieve"]

title: New York Style Pizza
description: The perfect slice: large, thin, and pliable enough to fold. Featuring a high-gluten crust that is crispy on the outside and chewy in the middle, topped with a simple, herb-rich tomato sauce and low-moisture mozzarella.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"High-Gluten Flour","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"at least 12% protein"},{"name":"Low-Moisture Mozzarella","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"grated at home"},{"name":"San Marzano Tomatoes","qty":"1","unit":"can","notes":"crushed with dried oregano"},{"name":"Olive Oil","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for the crust texture"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Cold Ferment**: Mix the dough and let it rest in the fridge for 48-72 hours. This develops the signature 'NY' flavor profile.
2. **The Stretch**: Use your hands to stretch the dough into a 16-inch circle. The center should be translucent, with a thicker edge (the cornicione).
3. **The Bake**: Use a pre-heated pizza stone or steel at the highest possible temperature. Bake for 8-10 minutes until the bottom is spotted with 'leopard' marks and the cheese is bubbling and slightly charred.
cookwareJson: ["Pizza stone or steel", "Pizza peel", "Large mixing bowl"]

title: Gazpacho Andaluz
description: Spain's chilled liquid salad. This isn't just tomato juice; it's a sophisticated emulsion of ultra-ripe tomatoes, peppers, cucumbers, and high-quality olive oil. Refreshing, vibrant, and essential for summer.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Ultra-ripe Roma Tomatoes","qty":"1","unit":"kg","notes":"the riper the better"},{"name":"Cucumber & Bell Pepper","qty":"1","unit":"each","notes":"peeled"},{"name":"Extra Virgin Olive Oil","qty":"100","unit":"ml","notes":"the best you have"},{"name":"Sherry Vinegar","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for the bite"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Blend**: Blend all vegetables with a splash of water and the vinegar. This creates a rough, chunky soup.
2. **The Emulsion**: While the blender is running on high, slowly drizzle in the olive oil. This turns the soup from red to a beautiful, creamy orange.
3. **The Chill**: Pass through a fine-mesh sieve for a silky texture. Chill for at least 2 hours. Serve in cold bowls with tiny garnishes of diced cucumber and tomato.

title: Feijoada (Brazilian Black Bean Stew)
description: Brazil's national treasure. A dark, rich, and intensely smoky stew of black beans and various cuts of pork. Traditionally served with white rice, collard greens, and farofa for a textural explosion.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Black Beans","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"dried, soaked overnight"},{"name":"Assorted Pork (Ribs, Sausage, Bacon)","qty":"1","unit":"kg","notes":"smoked cuts are best"},{"name":"Oranges","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"cut in half, simmered in the pot"},{"name":"Bay Leaves & Garlic","qty":"3","unit":"pcs","notes":"aromatics"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Beans**: Boil the soaked beans with the meat cuts and the whole oranges (the acidity helps tenderize the beans). Simmer for 2 hours until the beans are starting to break down.
2. **The Flavor Base**: Sauté a mountain of onions and garlic. Add 2 ladles of the cooked beans and mash them into a thick paste. Return this paste to the pot—it naturally thickens the stew.
3. **The Feast**: Serve the thick, black stew with bowls of farofa (toasted cassava flour) and orange slices to help with digestion.
cookwareJson: ["Large heavy stock pot", "Frying pan", "Wooden spoon for mashing"]

title: Lamb Tagine with Apricots
description: A Moroccan masterpiece of sweet and savory balance. Tender pieces of lamb shoulder are slow-cooked in a conical clay pot with honey, spice-laden apricots, and toasted almonds.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Lamb Shoulder","qty":"1","unit":"kg","notes":"cut into cubes"},{"name":"Dried Apricots","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"soaked in warm water"},{"name":"Ras el Hanout","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"Morocco's 'top of the shop' spice mix"},{"name":"Honey & Cinnamon","qty":"1","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for the glaze"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Spice Rub**: Apply Ras el Hanout and salt to the lamb. Let it sit for 30 minutes. Sauté onions and ginger in the tagine base.
2. **The Slow Braise**: Add lamb and stock. Cover with the conical lid. The shape allows steam to condense and drip back onto the meat, ensuring it never dries out.
3. **The Glaze**: In the last 30 minutes, add the apricots and honey. Simmer with the lid off until the sauce reduces to a thick, syrupy consistency. Top with toasted almonds and fresh cilantro.
cookwareJson: ["Clay Tagine or Dutch oven", "Small pan for almonds"]

title: Bibimbap
description: The ultimate balanced meal in a bowl. A canvas of white rice topped with individually sautéed 'namul' (seasoned vegetables), a sunny-side-up egg, and a dollop of spicy, sweet gochujang sauce.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Steamed Rice","qty":"2","unit":"cups","notes":"short grain"},{"name":"Spinach, Bean Sprouts, Shiitake","qty":"100","unit":"g each","notes":"individually prepared"},{"name":"Beef Bulgogi","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"thinly sliced"},{"name":"Gochujang Paste","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"mixed with sesame oil and sugar"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Namul**: Sauté each vegetable separately with a touch of sesame oil and garlic. Keeping them separate is key to the aesthetic and the variation in flavor.
2. **The Bulgogi**: Flash-sear the beef over high heat. It should be caramelized and tender.
3. **The Assembly**: Place rice in the bottom of a bowl (a hot Dolsot stone bowl is best if you have one). Arrange the colorful vegetables in a circle on top. Place the egg in the center. Serve with the gochujang sauce and mix EVERYTHING together thoroughly before eating.
cookwareJson: ["Stone bowl (Dolsot) or large ceramic bowl", "Small frying pan", "Rice cooker"]
